div.h3: Treiber Stack with Tagged Pointer (16 bytes)

ol.li:
Version always increments on each successful CAS
Prevents ABA: even if ptr recycles, version differs

16-byte atomic CAS compares BOTH fields atomically
Uses cmpxchg16b on x86-64

Linearization point = successful CAS
All threads agree on operation order at this instant

Memory ordering:
Acquire on load: see prior writes
Release on CAS success: publish our writes
Acquire on CAS failure: see winner's writes

div.p: Empty Stack Check: If old_top.ptr == NULL, the stack is empty. Return failure immediately without any CAS operation. In this case, stack is NOT empty.

Key Insight: Read next BEFORE CAS attempt. After successful CAS, another thread might pop again and the node could be retired. Reading while protected ensures safe access. Version increments to 6 to prevent ABA problem.

ol.li: Compares stack->top with old_top (BOTH version AND ptr, 16 bytes)
If equal: stores new_top, returns true
If not equal: updates old_top to current value, returns false Weak variant allows spurious failure in loops (more efficient on ARM).

div.p: Result: ✓ success = true ✓ value = 42 ✓ Version incremented: 5 → 6 ✓ Node A orphaned (retired to M4) Memory Safety (M4): Node A is retired, not freed. Scan will free when no hazard pointers protect it.
